content: Mei from Globex reached out via LinkedIn. Initial convo: they're evaluating cognitive memory solutions for agent workflows. Budget exists, timeline aggressive (wants pilot by Q2). Champion signal strong.
summary: Globex CTO Mei initiates contact, signals budget + aggressive timeline

content: First formal call with Mei + her colleague Derek (Procurement). Mei excited about our architecture; Derek immediately asked about SOC2 cert. We don't have it. Told him 4–6 week timeline. Deal stalled there.
summary: Globex procurement wall: SOC2 certification required, we're 4–6 weeks out

content: Globex deal blocker: SOC2 Type II cert + compliance questionnaire. All enterprise opportunities this size require it. Recommend we prioritize cert completion—impacts multiple pipelines, not just Globex.
summary: SOC2 is systematic blocker for enterprise deals; prioritize immediately

content: Anya flagged Globex in pipeline review. Estimated ACV: $180k/year if pilot converts. Devon assigned to kick off pre-sales technical deepdive with Mei (agents + memory layer architecture).
summary: Globex pipeline: $180k ACV, Devon leading technical scoping

content: When managing multi-call enterprise cycles with procurement friction: doc every blocker in Linear issue, tag Anya + Marcus, set weekly async check-in cadence. Prevents context decay and keeps deal visible to exec team.
summary: Enterprise deal tracking: Linear + async cadence beats ad-hoc emails

content: Devon + Mei call. Globex's use case: multi-agent orchestration with persistent conversation memory. Devon said architecture fit is 90%+. Mei asked for custom integration plan. Devon committed to draft by Feb 1.
summary: Globex technical fit strong; Devon to draft custom integration plan

content: Sebastien asked about Globex in 1-1. Told him SOC2 cert is the hard blocker, not tech. He suggested offering interim compliance statement while we finish audit. Escalated to Anya to explore feasibility.
summary: CEO suggests interim compliance statement as SOC2 bridge; Anya investigating

content: Call with Mei + Derek. Devon's integration plan delivered on time. Derek still wants full SOC2 before pilot contract. Also requested reference customer doing similar work. Told him we have 2 (confidential for now).
summary: Globex: integration plan delivered, Derek still blocking on SOC2 + refs

content: Anya reported: SOC2 audit at 65% complete. Auditor expects draft report by mid-March. Told Marcus to prep reference calls with Acme + Initech so we're ready if Globex asks (they will).
summary: SOC2 audit 65% done, mid-March draft expected; preping refs for Globex

content: Globex sale structure: Mei is technical champion, Derek owns procurement gate. Can't close without both. Mei pushes technical fit; Derek controls compliance risk. Strategy: satisfy Derek on SOC2 + compliance, keep Mei engaged on roadmap.
summary: Two-gate model for Globex: champion Mei + gatekeeper Derek require separate plays

content: Mei called (unscheduled). Globex exec team pushing hard on timeline. Wants pilot contract signed by end of Q1. Mei's credibility tied to this. Told her: SOC2 draft by March 15, legal review by March 22, contract ready April 1.
summary: Mei under pressure for Q1 close; committed to April 1 contract readiness

content: SOC2 auditor delivered draft report March 14 (one day early). Anya fast-tracked legal review. Derek from Globex asked if we could share preliminary findings. Yes, with NDA. Shared draft March 16.
summary: SOC2 draft delivered early; shared with Globex under NDA by March 16

content: Derek scheduled security architecture review call after reviewing SOC2 draft. Brought in their own InfoSec lead (Lisa). Call lasted 90 min. Derek seemed satisfied. Said: 'This addresses our major concerns.' Momentum shift visible.
summary: Globex security review post-SOC2 draft; Derek signals satisfaction, momentum builds

content: Mei + Derek joint call. Derek: 'Ready to move to contract phase.' Legal team starting procurement review. Derek estimated 2–3 weeks for final approval. Mei asked about pilot scope. Devon to send proposal addendum.
summary: Globex moves to contract phase; legal review 2–3 weeks, pilot scope TBD

content: James (new senior backend) assigned to Globex pilot implementation. Anya set budget at $45k for Q2 engineering work. Mei wants 90-day pilot starting May 1. James flagged: needs Priya to hire 1 contractor (vector DB specialist). Done.
summary: Globex pilot scoped: $45k budget, May 1 start, James leading, contractor hired

content: Globex legal came back with 47 comments on contract. Most minor; 3 major: data residency clause, IP on fine-tuned models, SLA uptime. Handed to Anya + Sebastien. Anya said: 'Doable, but need to negotiate residency.'
summary: Globex legal review: 47 comments, 3 major blockers including data residency

content: Closed negotiations on data residency (Globex wants US-only; we committed Snowflake + AWS regions only). IP clause simplified: we retain model weights, they get deployment rights. Derek approved. Expected signature by April 28.
summary: Globex legal resolved; data residency + IP settled, signature end of April

content: This deal taught me: long enterprise cycles aren't just sales friction—they're ops friction. Every week we don't have SOC2 cert, every ambiguous reference, every unsigned contract blocks engineering and recruiting decisions. I need to flag these upstream faster, not wait for CEO 1-1.
summary: Lesson: ops visibility on enterprise blockers must drive exec prioritization

content: Globex deal outcome: $180k/year contract signed, 90-day pilot May–July, Q3 renewal decision, $45k implementation cost, legal/procurement took 12 weeks. Root blocker was SOC2 cert, not product fit. Champion (Mei) was crucial for timeline pressure on procurement (Derek).
summary: Globex closed: $180k ACV, 90-day pilot, 12-week cycle, SOC2 was real blocker
